历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for science (physics category) was approximately 10717 → 19111 → 11908 → 20357 → 69792. In 2025, there was a significant gap (the geography major group dropped to 69792), while the normal major group minimum was about 17836. Over the past 4 years, normal rankings fluctuated between 10717 and 20357, with the 2025 gap being an anomaly. The provincial science enrollment plan was 49 in 2021–2022, 53 in 2023, 54 in 2024, and 50 in 2025. The 2026 plan is 57, a 14% increase from 2025, slightly easing competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 and Double First-Class university. On the Soft Science main ranking, it was approximately 71st in 2026, with a steady rise from 83rd in recent years, indicating steadily improving comprehensive strength. In 2025, reputation was somewhat negative, mainly due to poor employment data (the School of Life Sciences had an employment rate of only 4.63%), which attracted attention. On Douyin, its overall popularity ranked 62nd (average), though it reached a high of 11th in 2024, indicating moderate exposure. No significant enrollment gaps were recorded. It has A+ in Ethnology, A in Ecology, and A- in Statistics, directly related to majors such as Statistics, Environmental Science and Engineering, and Biological Sciences.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Engineering majors such as Computer Science and Electronic Information continue to have high social popularity. In 2026, the total number of examinees in the province is about 167,000 (a slight decrease of about 3% from 172,114 in 2025), slightly reducing competition, with recent years remaining stable.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering that the 2025 gap was an anomaly (the geography major group lowered the overall ranking), and with a 14% enrollment increase in 2026 and a slight decrease in examinee numbers, the ranking is expected to rebound significantly from the low gap to the normal range. However, due to the negative reputation and employment sentiment, the rebound will be limited. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 68089, with a range of 67589–68589.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for liberal arts (history category) was approximately 1516 → 6863 → 2554 → 2373 → 3342, showing an overall fluctuating and backward trend; in 2022, due to batch merger, the ranking significantly dropped (6863); in the recent 3 years, it stabilized in the range of 2373–3342; the 2025 baseline is approximately 3342; the provincial liberal arts plan was 24 students in both 2021 and 2022, 20 in 2023, 19 in 2024, and 15 in 2025; the 2026 plan is 14 students, a reduction of about 6.7% compared to 2025, leading to slightly intensified competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211, Double First-Class; ranked approximately 71st in the 2026 Soft Science main ranking, with a continuous upward trend in recent years, steadily improving overall strength; reputation in 2025 was slightly negative, mainly due to poor employment data attracting attention; Douyin popularity ranked 62nd overall (average), with moderate overall exposure; no obvious record of admission cutoff gaps; Ethnology A+, Ecology A, Statistics A-, indirectly related to liberal arts majors such as Chinese Language and Literature, History, and Law.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Social popularity of liberal arts majors such as Law and Chinese Language and Literature remains stable; the total number of examinees in the province in 2026 is approximately 167,000 (a slight decrease of about 3% compared to 2025), with history category examinees estimated at about 52,772 (a decrease of about 3.5% from 54,707 in 2025), leading to slightly reduced competition, remaining stable in recent years.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the fluctuating and backward trend of historical rankings, the slight reduction in the 2026 plan, and the slight decrease in provincial examinees, based on the 2025 baseline of 3342, competition is expected to intensify slightly but to a limited extent; the predicted minimum ranking is approximately 3228, with a range of 2728–3728.
